title: Do Everything As to the Lord
reading:
Colossians 3:22-23 brings it home with striking simplicity: not with eyeservice, as men-pleasers, but in sincerity of heart, fearing God. Whatever you do, do it heartily, as to the Lord and not to men.

Eyeservice — working hard when you're being watched, and coasting when you're not. Performing for the audience in front of you. It is one of the most common forms of people pleasing, and Paul names it directly. The antidote is not trying harder. It is redirecting your audience.

When you work, live, and serve as to the Lord — He becomes your primary witness. You are no longer managing perceptions or measuring responses. You are simply bringing your whole heart to what is in front of you, as an act of worship to the one who sees everything.

This is what freedom from people pleasing looks like in practice. Not indifference to others. Not refusing all feedback. But doing everything from a place of God's approval already secured — not working toward it, working from it.

You are chosen. You are loved. You are made in His image, sealed by His Spirit, and declared worthy by the blood of Jesus. You do not need the crowd's vote. God has already spoken.

Do it heartily. Do it as to the Lord.
reflection: What would your work, relationships, and daily life look like if you did everything as to the Lord rather than to people?
abide: Lord, free me from the need for approval that You have already given. Teach me to live and love and work as to You — because Your word over me is enough.
